answer: `STR2ET` converts a string representing an epoch to a double precision value representing the number of TDB seconds past the J2000 epoch [file:3.0-1483.0]. It parses the input string `TIMSTR` using `TPARTV` [file:119.0-123.0], handles time zone and system defaults [file:153.0-218.0], and processes the string based on its format, including Julian dates and calendar strings [file:222.0-226.0]. If the input string cannot be recognized as a legitimate time string, the error `SPICE(UNPARSEDTIME)` is signaled [file:58.0-60.0].
